SEARCH

Class Graph

java.lang.Object
Graph
All Implemented Interfaces:
GraphInterface<Town,Road>
public class Graph extends Object implements GraphInterface<Town,Road>

  • Constructor Details

    • Graph

      public Graph()

  • Method Details

    • getEdge

      public Road getEdge(Town source, Town destination)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Returns an edge connecting source vertex to target vertex if such vertices and such edge exist in this graph. Otherwise returns null. If any of the specified vertices is null returns null In undirected graphs, the returned edge may have its source and target vertices in the opposite order.
      Specified by:
      getEdge in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      source - source vertex of the edge.
      destination - target vertex of the edge.
      Returns:
      an edge connecting source vertex to target vertex.

    • addEdge

      public Road addEdge(Town source, Town destination, int weight, String description)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Creates a new edge in this graph, going from the source vertex to the target vertex, and returns the created edge. The source and target vertices must already be contained in this graph. If they are not found in graph IllegalArgumentException is thrown.
      Specified by:
      addEdge in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      source - source vertex of the edge.
      destination - target vertex of the edge.
      weight - weight of the edge
      description - description for edge
      Returns:
      The newly created edge if added to the graph, otherwise null.

    • dijkstraShortestPath

      public void dijkstraShortestPath(Town sourceVertex)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Dijkstra's Shortest Path Method. Internal structures are built which hold the ability to retrieve the path, shortest distance from the sourceVertex to all the other vertices in the graph, etc.
      Specified by:
      dijkstraShortestPath in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      sourceVertex - the vertex to find shortest path from

    • shortestPath

      public ArrayList<String> shortestPath(Town sourceVertex, Town destinationVertex)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Find the shortest path from the sourceVertex to the destinationVertex call the dijkstraShortestPath with the sourceVertex
      Specified by:
      shortestPath in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      sourceVertex - starting vertex
      destinationVertex - ending vertex
      Returns:
      An arraylist of Strings that describe the path from sourceVertex to destinationVertex

    • addVertex

      public boolean addVertex(Town v)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Adds the specified vertex to this graph if not already present. More formally, adds the specified vertex, v, to this graph if this graph contains no vertex u such that u.equals(v). If this graph already contains such vertex, the call leaves this graph unchanged and returns false. In combination with the restriction on constructors, this ensures that graphs never contain duplicate vertices.
      Specified by:
      addVertex in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      v - vertex to be added to this graph.
      Returns:
      true if this graph did not already contain the specified vertex.

    • containsEdge

      public boolean containsEdge(Town sourceVertex, Town destinationVertex)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Returns true if and only if this graph contains an edge going from the source vertex to the target vertex. In undirected graphs the same result is obtained when source and target are inverted. If any of the specified vertices does not exist in the graph, or if is null, returns false.
      Specified by:
      containsEdge in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      sourceVertex - source vertex of the edge.
      destinationVertex - target vertex of the edge.
      Returns:
      true if this graph contains the specified edge.

    • containsVertex

      public boolean containsVertex(Town v)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Returns true if this graph contains the specified vertex. More formally, returns true if and only if this graph contains a vertex u such that u.equals(v). If the specified vertex is null returns false.
      Specified by:
      containsVertex in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      v - vertex whose presence in this graph is to be tested.
      Returns:
      true if this graph contains the specified vertex.

    • edgeSet

      public Set<Road> edgeSet()
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Returns a set of the edges contained in this graph. The set is backed by the graph, so changes to the graph are reflected in the set. If the graph is modified while an iteration over the set is in progress, the results of the iteration are undefined.
      Specified by:
      edgeSet in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Returns:
      a set of the edges contained in this graph.

    • edgesOf

      public Set<Road> edgesOf(Town vertex)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Returns a set of all edges touching the specified vertex (also referred to as adjacent vertices). If no edges are touching the specified vertex returns an empty set.
      Specified by:
      edgesOf in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      vertex - the vertex for which a set of touching edges is to be returned.
      Returns:
      a set of all edges touching the specified vertex.

    • removeEdge

      public Road removeEdge(Town sourceVertex, Town destinationVertex, int weight, String description)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Removes an edge going from source vertex to target vertex, if such vertices and such edge exist in this graph. If weight >- 1 it must be checked If description != null, it must be checked Returns the edge if removed or null otherwise.
      Specified by:
      removeEdge in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      sourceVertex - source vertex of the edge.
      destinationVertex - target vertex of the edge.
      weight - weight of the edge
      description - description of the edge
      Returns:
      The removed edge, or null if no edge removed.

    • removeVertex

      public boolean removeVertex(Town v)
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Removes the specified vertex from this graph including all its touching edges if present. More formally, if the graph contains a vertex u such that u.equals(v), the call removes all edges that touch u and then removes u itself. If no such u is found, the call leaves the graph unchanged. Returns true if the graph contained the specified vertex. (The graph will not contain the specified vertex once the call returns). If the specified vertex is null returns false.
      Specified by:
      removeVertex in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Parameters:
      v - vertex to be removed from this graph, if present.
      Returns:
      true if the graph contained the specified vertex; false otherwise.

    • vertexSet

      public Set<Town> vertexSet()
      Description copied from interface: GraphInterface
      Returns a set of the vertices contained in this graph. The set is backed by the graph, so changes to the graph are reflected in the set. If the graph is modified while an iteration over the set is in progress, the results of the iteration are undefined.
      Specified by:
      vertexSet in interface GraphInterface<Town,Road>
      Returns:
      a set view of the vertices contained in this graph.

    • getTowns

      public Set<Town> getTowns()
      getter method for the towns in the graph
      Returns:
      the towns

    • setTowns

      public void setTowns(Set<Town> _towns)
      setter method for the towns of the graph
      Parameters:
      _towns - the towns to set

    • getRoads

      public Set<Road> getRoads()
      getter method for the roads in a graph
      Returns:
      the roads

    • setRoads

      public void setRoads(Set<Road> _roads)
      setter method for the roads in a graph
      Parameters:
      _roads - the roads to set
